behavior: Primarily a ground forager, scratching through leaf litter with both feet; often concealed in dense undergrowth and may perch in low shrubs to sing.
field_marks: Large, chunky sparrow with a thick triangular bill
Long, rounded tail with conspicuous white outer corners
Adult male has a sooty-black head, throat, breast, and upperparts
Warm rufous sides contrast with a clean white belly
Folded wing shows small white bars or a white check-mark
Eyes are usually dark to reddish; legs are pinkish
habitat: Dense brush, tangles, thickets, scrub, and forest edges with leaf litter for ground foraging.

sources.title: Eastern Towhee Identification — All About Birds
